examples: They would also terrorize and kill dogs and cats which is not what the suburban dwellers have in mind.
Good landladies—those who are superb cooks and launderers, are figures as popular in fiction as the bad ones who terrorize their guests and overcharge them at the slightest opportunity.
They wanted to terrorize us, but, as Americans, we refuse to live in fear.
Speed, power, balance and a searing shot with his left foot combine to terrorize defenses.
In the Central African Republic, the agency reports that armed gangs terrorize farms and communities, kidnapping children and holding them for ransom.
